matches.match_rationale: Each campus exports performance data in its own schema — different column names, different calculation logic for retention or pass rate, different granularity. AI-assisted ETL can map these heterogeneous inputs to a single canonical schema, flagging ambiguous fields for human review. This directly addresses the 'campuses tracking different metrics' and 'pass rate calculated differently' sub-problems, which are fundamentally data normalization problems.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pipeline that ingests raw exports from each campus SIS/LMS, uses AI schema mapping to normalize field names and calculation logic, and outputs a unified tabular dataset ready for a shared dashboard — reducing manual reconciliation from days to hours.

matches.match_rationale: Once normalized data exists, HQ needs narrative performance summaries that contextualize campus-level KPIs for board reports, accreditation submissions, and leadership reviews. Without consistent metrics, these narratives are either avoided or require manual drafting per campus. AI text generation can produce standardized comparative narrative reports from structured metric tables, enabling consistent framing of performance across sites.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A monthly report generator that takes the normalized KPI table and produces a structured narrative — 'Campus A retention improved 3pp YoY, above network average; Campus B graduation rate lags benchmark by 8pp' — for board packs and internal reviews.

matches.match_rationale: Once a normalized metric dataset exists, structured classification can segment campuses into performance tiers or flag underperformers across multiple KPI dimensions simultaneously. This addresses HQ's 'lacks visibility into actual comparative performance' by surfacing which campuses need intervention rather than just presenting raw numbers that leaders must manually interpret.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A campus performance tiering model that classifies each campus as high/medium/at-risk on each KPI dimension, updating each reporting cycle, and feeding a simple HQ dashboard with red/amber/green indicators.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end process of collecting campus data exports, normalizing schemas, computing standardized KPIs, and populating a shared dashboard is a repeatable multi-step workflow currently done manually or not at all. A task automation agent can orchestrate the full pipeline — pulling data from campus systems, applying transformation rules, computing agreed KPI definitions, and pushing results to a central reporting layer — on a scheduled cadence.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated monthly pipeline agent that connects to each campus's data source, applies standardized KPI calculation logic, and populates a shared Power BI or Looker dashboard without manual intervention from HQ analytics staff.
